Name: -1_to_and_the_for
Representative_Docs: ['I know that I do not have a lot of extra money to spend on food, so I try to buy just what I need and have foods that go a long way and do not cost as much. This does not effect my work very much, but I do have an extra job in addition to my full time job which I use for groceries throughout the week to lessen my stress over affording food. I have less free time and sometimes go to bed much later than I would want because I get stuck at my other job.', " Offer regular food service with discounted (affordable)and/or free meals. Loss of cental food services have left many feeling isolated and students leave campus as soon a class is over, since there is no longer a place to visit with friends and eat on a regular basis.  State would need to address these solutions: full coverage of tuition, fees and books to free up funding for living expenses. Housing needs to be addressed by the city and state. While dorms are not usually considered for community colleges, I think if SFCC could offer limited dormitories for students who are required to spend large amounts of time in person on campus such as nursing students. I also think it would be worth exploring if the nearby IAIA campus has any empty dorm rooms that could be made available to SFCC students, but we'd need to find funds to help students with those expenses. It would also be helpful if we had more emergency funds available to students who need to move, etc. We do offer some limited funds via the SFCC Foundation, but I don't think they are authorized to give the amounts that would be needed to pay first and last month's deposit before someone is evicted. Also, if the state could assist with offering covered or more affordable health insurance for employees it would free up money for food and housing expenses. In general, rental options are very expensive and limited. Santa Fe seniors are on the waiting list for housing assistance for years. Young people do not have access to any inexpensive housing options. Santa Fe is slowly becoming a community where the poor and middle class have no where to live. ", "Right now we are doing a good job starting up the food pantry for students, and working on a community garden.  I know that some of my co-workers either work or have had to work multiple jobs in order to live sustainably, so having more conversations about that and coming up with solutions would be good.  While having good food security at home is the best option, I think having a cafeteria with consistent hours and reasonable food prices could potentially be helpful for our student and staff population.  As for housing, we don't offer anything that I am aware of to address that issue.  We can only point others to outside resources that are available."]

The fact that faculty get bonuses and incentives and annual raises every single year, whereas staff has only seen an increase in the last couple of years is absurd. Name: 47_santa_fe_mexico_high
Representative_Docs: ['The cost of living in Santa Fe is much to high. Almost everyone has to commute to work here.', "I hope the state legislature will prioritize higher education as a way for students to gain access to better paying jobs as well as become more rounded and informed citizens. I'm glad elementary education and high school education are being more supported. But individuals working in higher education in New Mexico do not feel valued. I am concerned since I've seen staff and faculty leave the college to go work in the elementary and high schools since they can earn more pay there. They have more steps to earn higher wages, etc. in the lower-level schools. Our college needs to retain quality staff, adjunct and full-time faculty to meet the challenges in our community. ", 'Housing costs are too high for wages in NM.  Renting or owning...a one bedroom apartment in an average neighborhood costing $1400 or a mortgage costing 1400.  My take home pay does not allow for me to spend that much.  I rent from a friend of the family and they discounted the rent so I could stay there long term.  I do not have a vehicle payment.  I do not travel nor spend much on entertainment.  NM has never been good to me about wages versus cost of living.  I have always struggled in this state.  I am a graduate of NMSU and I have a masters degree.  I hope this helps.  I would like to ask the State of NM to think about adding the Roadrunner train to Socorro or a charter bus.  We currently use van pools and car pools.  I would like to ride the train.']

Name: 63_transportation_bus_route_shuttle
Representative_Docs: ["I rely on ABQ city busses to get to/from work on UNM main campus. Reliability of the bus line I take (790) has become increasingly unreliable, especially for the morning route. The bus is often late to pick us up, or won't show up at all. ", 'The city of Albuquerque has decreased bus services; they discontinued a route that would place me within walking distance of my home. I now must ride later bus and walk in the dark home at lease 1-2 times a week. The commuter routes feel safer than the Lomas route (example) I have had two scary rides with people who had outbursts and were threatening to the bus driver and other passengers. the city bus is much more focused on down-town workers with regular work hours and not UNM workers who have later and earlier working hours that are not consistent. I  have only one car and share with my spouse so that I can afford for my disabled son to have transportation; I pay for the least expensive UNM parking, but park in the lot only about once a week. It is expensive parking for just once a week.', "I appreciate that this survey exists. I think the public transportation piece is really important. We were excited about the Blue Bus, but haven't heard any updates. Is that still happening? It would be great if we could have a UNM-Taos shuttle that goes into town. It would be great advertising for UNM-Taos and could maybe even increase enrollment. "]
